body_txt: A shuriken (throwing star) which is really, really big. Fuuma literally translates as "wind demon" and is taken from the name of a famous Ninja clan. Mostly used by ninja for thematic purposes, despite it having lost the quality which made it useful to them in the first place . Can sometimes be folded for easier storage . The Fuuma Shuriken is a common weapon for characters that can control and weaponize wind , which often are ninjas to begin with, down to the Fuuma clan that gives this trope its name. Sometimes, having wind powers is the Required Secondary Power to make the giant shuriken even fly at all. Since a shuriken looks somewhat like a propeller, a Transforming Mecha that turns into a helicopter can wield or launch the blades as a makeshift Fuuma Shuriken. Sometimes overlaps with Precision-Guided Boomerang . Compare Deadly Disc and Rings of Death . A Sub-Trope of Stock Ninja Weaponry , Bigger Is Better , and Impossibly Cool Weapon (try to use it without cutting yourself in two). Not to be confused with Fūma Kotarō (though he has wielded them in fiction).
